notes: Pricing researched from public sources as of March 2025. Spot/preemptible prices are approximate and fluctuate. TPU spot discount is typically ~60-70% off on-demand. AWS Trainium spot availability is limited.

tpu_v5e.notes: Widely available. Best for inference and smaller training jobs. Spot ~70% discount typical for TPUs.

tpu_v5p.notes: High-performance training chip. Large HBM capacity for big models.

tpu_v6e_trillium.notes: GA as of 2025. 4.7x peak compute vs v5e. ~2x inference throughput vs v5e. 67% more energy efficient than v5e. Some sources report on-demand as low as $1.375/chip-hr which may reflect a different config or region.

pricing_per_instance_hour_usd.spot_notes: Spot varies widely by region: us-east-2 (Ohio) ~$2.15, us-east-1 (Virginia) ~$6.78, us-west-2 (Oregon) ~$11.01

trn1.notes: 16 Trainium chips with NeuronLink-v2 interconnect (2D torus). 128 vCPUs, 512 GiB RAM, 8x100 Gbps EFA.

pricing_per_instance_hour_usd.on_demand_notes: CONFLICTING DATA across sources. Spare Cores reports ~$16.02/hr, BigBell reports ~$85.96/hr, one blog estimates ~$4.80/hr (likely outdated/incorrect). Limited regional availability (us-east-2 only as of early 2026). Treat all figures as approximate until AWS publishes stable public pricing.
pricing_per_instance_hour_usd.spot_notes: Spot pricing not widely available as of March 2026.

pricing_per_chip_hour_usd.on_demand_notes: Derived from instance price / 16 chips. Range reflects conflicting source data.

trn2.notes: 16 Trainium2 chips with NeuronLink. 192 vCPUs, 2048 GiB RAM, 16x200 Gbps networking. 1.5 TB HBM3 total. Very limited availability (us-east-2 only). AWS claims 30-40% better price-performance than H100-based P5e instances.

tpu_v5e_per_chip.notes: Per-chip throughput from PyTorch/XLA, may reflect different batch/sequence config than the 8-chip number

tpu_v6e_trillium_per_node.notes: ~2x throughput vs v5e. At low concurrency ~120 tok/s.

trainium2_trn2.notes: No public Llama 70B token/sec benchmarks found for Trainium2 as of March 2026. AWS claims 30-40% better price-perf vs H100 P5e and up to 54% lower cost-per-token for GPT-class models, but without published tok/sec figures.
AWS claims 3x higher token-generation throughput vs other major cloud providers for Llama 405B on Bedrock, but absolute numbers not published.

best_perf_per_dollar_inference.notes: Highest throughput per dollar for Llama 70B class inference based on available data

highest_raw_throughput.notes: Long-sequence inference. H100 ~150 tok/s per GPU for comparison.
